member.name: T:Tanuden.Rudolf.Adapters.BVE.BveAdapterStatus
member.summary: Diagnostic snapshot of the BVE bridge reader, surfaced through under the bve:bridgeStatus key. Analogous to the TrainCrew adapter's ApiStatus.

member.name: P:Tanuden.Rudolf.Adapters.BVE.BveAdapterStatus.FrameConnected
member.summary: True when the frame MMF is open (mapped).

member.name: P:Tanuden.Rudolf.Adapters.BVE.BveAdapterStatus.ProfileConnected
member.summary: True when the profile MMF is open (mapped).

member.name: P:Tanuden.Rudolf.Adapters.BVE.BveAdapterStatus.LastFrameGeneration
member.summary: Latest accepted frame generation, or 0 if none yet.

member.name: P:Tanuden.Rudolf.Adapters.BVE.BveAdapterStatus.LastFrameAgeMs
member.summary: Milliseconds since the frame generation last advanced; null if no frame ever read.

member.name: P:Tanuden.Rudolf.Adapters.BVE.BveAdapterStatus.IgnoredCommandCount
member.summary: Count of s dropped by the output-only bridge.

member.name: P:Tanuden.Rudolf.Adapters.BVE.BveAdapterStatus.LastError
member.summary: Most recent open/read error message; null when healthy.

member.name: T:Tanuden.Rudolf.Adapters.BVE.BveRudolfAdapter
member.summary:
Reads BVE Trainsim state from the Minatogawa bridge's memory-mapped-file transport and exposes it as Rudolf documents. The bridge (a BVE-EX plugin running inside BVE) already performs all BVE -> Rudolf translation and writes finished / JSON; this adapter is purely the reader side of that IPC.
Reads are on-demand with a generation cache: each call does a lock-free seqlock read and only re-deserialises when the bridge's generation counter advances. Liveness is time-based (the mapping outlives BVE while we hold a handle), so reflects whether the generation has advanced recently rather than merely whether the file is open.
Single consumer poll thread assumed; see .

member.name: F:Tanuden.Rudolf.Adapters.BVE.Internal.BveBridgeConstants.HeaderBytes
member.summary: Framing header size: uint64 generation @0, uint32 payload length @8.

member.name: F:Tanuden.Rudolf.Adapters.BVE.Internal.BveBridgeConstants.GenerationOffset
member.summary: Offset of the uint64 generation counter (odd = write in progress, even = stable).

member.name: F:Tanuden.Rudolf.Adapters.BVE.Internal.BveBridgeConstants.LengthOffset
member.summary: Offset of the uint32 payload length.

member.name: T:Tanuden.Rudolf.Adapters.BVE.Internal.BveMmfChannel
member.summary:
One reader-side channel over a single Minatogawa bridge MMF (profile or frame). Owns the memory-mapped file + view, performs the lock-free seqlock read, and tracks liveness via the generation counter's last advance.
Not thread-safe: a single consumer poll thread is assumed (matching the host's loop). If the host ever polls from multiple threads, callers must serialise access to a channel.

member.name: P:Tanuden.Rudolf.Adapters.BVE.Internal.BveMmfChannel.IsOpen
member.summary: True once the MMF exists and a view is mapped.

member.name: P:Tanuden.Rudolf.Adapters.BVE.Internal.BveMmfChannel.LastGeneration
member.summary: The most recent stable generation accepted, or 0 if none yet.

member.name: P:Tanuden.Rudolf.Adapters.BVE.Internal.BveMmfChannel.LastError
member.summary: Message from the most recent failed , or null when healthy.

member.name: P:Tanuden.Rudolf.Adapters.BVE.Internal.BveMmfChannel.LastAdvanceUtc
member.summary: Wall-clock of the last generation advance, or null if the channel never produced a frame.

member.name: M:Tanuden.Rudolf.Adapters.BVE.Internal.BveMmfChannel.IsLive(System.Int64)
member.summary: True when the channel is open and the generation has advanced within . Because the kernel keeps the mapping alive while we hold a handle (even after BVE exits), a time-based check is the only reliable signal that the writer is still running.

member.name: M:Tanuden.Rudolf.Adapters.BVE.Internal.BveMmfChannel.EnsureOpen
member.summary: Lazy open. Tolerates "bridge not running yet" ( throws until the writer creates the MMF); the next call retries. Returns true once a read-only view is mapped.

member.name: M:Tanuden.Rudolf.Adapters.BVE.Internal.BveMmfChannel.TryReadFramed(System.Byte[]@,System.Boolean@)
member.summary: Seqlock read. On a fresh, consistent frame sets to the UTF-8 JSON and to true. When the generation is unchanged since the last read, returns true with null and false (still connected, nothing new). Returns false when the channel can't be opened or a stable read couldn't be obtained within the bounded retries.
